creator: girgen@FreeBSD.org
text:
the bsd.apache.mk says this:

# Examples:
#  USE_APACHE= 22       # specify exact version
#  USE_APACHE= 22+      # specify [min] version, no [max] version
#  USE_APACHE= 22-24    # specify [min]-[max] range
#  USE_APACHE= -22      # specify [max] version, no [min] version


Since shibboleth probably will not support upcoming apache versions out of the box, I do think that the shibboleth port does the right thing here by setting 22-24. There are two options.

1. The 22-24 functionality is removed altogether, from bsd.apache.mk (and as a result from that, also from the shibboleth port)

2. The 22-24 functionality in bsd.apache.mk is fixed to work with poudriere.
